name: Alki Beach
blurb: Long cobble-and-sand beach on Elliott Bay; easy access and reliable intertidal life.

name: Constellation Park
blurb: Rocky reef beach below Alki Point lighthouse; excellent low-tide invertebrate diversity.

name: Lincoln Park
blurb: West Seattle park with a long rocky/cobble shoreline rich in intertidal critters.

name: Discovery Park / West Point
blurb: Sandy and cobble beaches below the bluff at the city's largest park.

name: Golden Gardens Park
blurb: Sandy beach with rocky pockets near Shilshole; good for a casual low-tide wander.

name: Carkeek Park
blurb: Wide flats below the railroad; cobble and sand with tidepool patches at extreme lows.

name: Richmond Beach Saltwater Park
blurb: Shoreline park with a broad gravel beach; classic Sound tidepooling spot.

name: Seahurst Park
blurb: Burien park with a restored beach and a marine education center; gentle flats.

name: Saltwater State Park
blurb: Des Moines park with a sheltered cove; accessible tidepools at low water.

name: Des Moines Beach Park
blurb: Creek-mouth beach park just north of the marina; easy family tidepooling.

name: Redondo Beach
blurb: Boardwalk beach in Des Moines with a MaST aquarium and gentle intertidal flats.

name: Dash Point State Park
blurb: Sandy beach with broad warm-water flats on the Federal Way shoreline.

name: Owen Beach (Point Defiance)
blurb: Tacoma's Point Defiance shoreline; gravel beach with views of Vashon.

name: Titlow Beach
blurb: Tacoma beach by the Narrows with a tide-fed lagoon and rich rocky intertidal.

name: Kopachuck State Park
blurb: Carr Inlet park with a long beach; clam flats and sea stars at low tide.

name: Penrose Point State Park
blurb: Key Peninsula park with sandspits and warm shallow flats on Mayo Cove.

name: Joemma Beach State Park
blurb: Quiet Key Peninsula beach on Case Inlet with tideflats and a dock.

name: Tolmie State Park
blurb: Olympia-area underwater park with sandy flats and an artificial reef.

name: Burfoot Park
blurb: Thurston County park on Budd Inlet with a forested bluff and beach access.

name: Squaxin (Priest Point) Park
blurb: Olympia city park on Budd Inlet with estuary trails and a soft-bottom beach.

name: Twanoh State Park
blurb: Warm Hood Canal park famous for oysters and clams on a gentle south-shore beach.

name: Potlatch State Park
blurb: Lower Hood Canal park with broad tideflats prized for shellfish.

name: Manchester State Park
blurb: Former fort on Rich Passage with a pebbly beach and strong tidal currents.

name: Fort Ward Park
blurb: Bainbridge park on Rich Passage; rocky shoreline and good shore diving.

name: Fay Bainbridge Park
blurb: North Bainbridge beach facing the main basin; driftwood and sandy gravel.

name: Illahee State Park
blurb: Port Orchard reach park with a saltwater beach and old-growth forest.

name: Scenic Beach State Park
blurb: Seabeck park on Hood Canal with oysters and Olympic Mountain views.

name: Kitsap Memorial State Park
blurb: Hood Canal park north of Poulsbo with a gravel beach and tideflats.

name: Point No Point
blurb: Hansville lighthouse point; cobble beach and a productive low-tide reef.

name: Foulweather Bluff Preserve
blurb: Nature Conservancy preserve at the mouth of Hood Canal; wild beach and lagoon.

name: Brackett's Landing (Edmonds)
blurb: Underwater park beside the ferry; an artificial reef alive with marine life.

name: Picnic Point Park
blurb: Edmonds-area beach park beneath the bluff with a pedestrian rail overpass.

name: Mukilteo Lighthouse Park
blurb: Beach park by the ferry dock with cobble flats and a historic lighthouse.

name: Howarth Park
blurb: Everett park with a footbridge over the tracks to a driftwood beach.

name: Kayak Point Regional Park
blurb: Snohomish County park on Port Susan with a long sandy beach and pier.

name: Double Bluff Beach
blurb: Long sandy spit on south Whidbey with towering bluffs and broad low-tide flats.

name: Fort Casey State Park
blurb: Admiralty Inlet fort with a lighthouse and a cobble beach in swift current.

name: Ebey's Landing
blurb: Bluff-trail beach on the open Strait; surf-washed cobble and sand.

name: Joseph Whidbey State Park
blurb: West Whidbey park with one of the island's best sandy beaches.

name: Rosario Beach (Deception Pass)
blurb: Deception Pass cove with rocky reefs and a Marine Health Observation Area.

name: Camano Island State Park
blurb: Saratoga Passage park with a rocky beach and clam-digging flats.

name: Cama Beach State Park
blurb: Historic camp on Camano with a gentle beach on Saratoga Passage.

name: Washington Park (Anacortes)
blurb: Fidalgo Island park with rocky shoreline, tidepools, and a loop drive.

name: Cattle Point (San Juan Island)
blurb: Rocky point at the south end of San Juan Island; strong currents, rich life.

name: South Beach (San Juan Island NHP)
blurb: The island's longest public beach; driftwood, sand, and open-Strait exposure.

name: Lime Kiln Point State Park
blurb: Whale-watch park on Haro Strait with rocky shore and a small lighthouse.

name: Jackson Beach (Friday Harbor)
blurb: Sheltered Friday Harbor beach with a lagoon and easy intertidal access.

name: Spencer Spit State Park
blurb: Lopez Island sandspit enclosing a saltwater lagoon; clams and shorebirds.

name: Odlin County Park
blurb: Lopez Island park near the ferry with a long sandy beach on the channel.

name: Larrabee State Park
blurb: Sandstone shelves on Samish Bay; tidepools tucked among the eroded rock.

name: Marine Park (Bellingham)
blurb: Fairhaven waterfront park with a pebble beach on Bellingham Bay.

name: Locust Beach
blurb: North Bellingham beach below the bluff with broad sandy low-tide flats.

name: Birch Bay State Park
blurb: Shallow warm bay near Blaine with extensive clam and crab flats.

name: Semiahmoo Park
blurb: Sandspit park near Blaine separating the bay from Semiahmoo's harbor.

name: Salt Creek Recreation Area
blurb: Tongue Point Marine Sanctuary near Port Angeles — among the best tidepools in WA.

name: Freshwater Bay
blurb: County park west of Port Angeles with a cobble beach and kelp-fringed reefs.

name: Fort Worden State Park
blurb: Port Townsend fort with a science center, lighthouse, and long Strait beach.

name: North Beach (Port Townsend)
blurb: Driftwood beach facing Admiralty Inlet with views to Whidbey and the Cascades.

name: Fort Flagler State Park
blurb: Marrowstone Island fort with sandspits and broad flats at the bay mouth.

name: Dungeness Spit (NWR)
blurb: One of the world's longest natural sandspits; a wildlife refuge with vast flats.

name: Sequim Bay State Park
blurb: Sheltered Sequim Bay park with a protected beach behind a natural spit.
